entries.text: Do not go with someone you do not know, even if they say they have a present or know your family. Stay where you can see a grown-up you trust. If someone you do not know tries to take you somewhere, say "No!" and tell a trusted adult right away.
question_hints: What if a stranger asks me to come

entries.text: When you cross the road, use a crossing if there is one. Look left, right, and left again before you step out. Hold a grown-up’s hand if you are young. Never run into the road.
question_hints: How do I cross the road

entries.text: Do not go into deep water or swim without a grown-up watching. Stay where you can stand if you are in a pool. If someone is in trouble in the water, tell a grown-up; do not jump in yourself.
Do not touch matches, lighters, or the stove. If you see fire or smoke, tell a grown-up right away. If there is a fire at home, get outside and go to your meeting place. Do not go back inside.

Do not tell people online your real name, address, or school unless a trusted grown-up says it is okay. Do not meet someone you only know from the internet without a grown-up. If something online feels wrong, tell a grown-up.
question_hints: Should I tell my name online

entries.text: Your body belongs to you. No one should touch you in a way that makes you feel uncomfortable. If someone does, say "No" or "Stop" and tell a trusted grown-up. It is never your fault.
question_hints: Someone touched me and I don't like it

entries.text: Medicine and pills can be dangerous if you take the wrong amount or take someone else’s. Only take medicine when a grown-up gives it to you. Do not eat or drink things from bottles under the sink or in cabinets.

In a real emergency, like someone badly hurt or a fire, a grown-up can call for help. In many places the number is 911 or 999. Know your full name and address so you can tell the person on the phone if a grown-up asks you to.
question_hints: What number do I call in emergency

entries.text: Do not approach or touch a dog or other animal you do not know without asking the owner. If a dog is loose and you are scared, stand still like a tree; do not run. Tell a grown-up about the animal.
question_hints: A dog is running at me
